mission.title: openeverest: Cannot create PostgreSQL cluster with ClusterIP expose type
mission.description: Creating a PostgreSQL database cluster via the UI fails with 'Unsupported value: ClusterIP: supported values: internal, external'. The UI incorrectly sends 'ClusterIP' as the proxy expose type instead of the expected 'internal' or 'external' values.

steps.title: Identify the error
steps.description:
Check for the error when creating a database cluster:
```bash
kubectl get events -n <database-namespace> --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | grep -i invalid
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=50 | grep -i clusterip
```
Look for the validation error: `spec.proxy.expose.type: Unsupported value: "ClusterIP"`.

steps.title: Apply the workaround
steps.description:
Create the database cluster using kubectl with the correct expose type value instead of the UI:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: everest.percona.com/v1alpha1
kind: DatabaseCluster
metadata:
  name: <cluster-name>
  namespace: <namespace>
spec:
  engine:
    type: postgresql
    version: "16"
    replicas: 1
    storage:
      size: 25Gi
  proxy:
    type: pgbouncer
    replicas: 1
    expose:
      type: internal
EOF
```

Use `internal` for ClusterIP-equivalent behavior (accessible only within the cluster) or `external` for LoadBalancer-equivalent behavior.

steps.title: Verify the database cluster is created
steps.description:
Check that the database cluster was created successfully:
```bash
kubectl get databasecluster -n <namespace>
kubectl get pods -n <namespace> -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<cluster-name>
```

resolution.summary: The OpenEverest UI sends 'ClusterIP' as the proxy expose type but the API only accepts 'internal' or 'external'. Use kubectl to create the cluster with type 'internal' (equivalent to ClusterIP) as a workaround until the UI is fixed.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with OpenEverest installed, attempting to create a PostgreSQL database cluster.
